heading: Mongoose Custom Setters
body:
Custom setters modify your data when it is saved, and can be implemented similar
to validators. Mongoose provides the following custom setters:

- `lowercase`

- `uppercase`

- `trim`

The following example lowercases the characters in the `blog.slug` field:

```javascript
const blogSchema = new Schema({
   ...
   slug:  {
      type: String,
      required: true,
      minLength: 4,
      lowercase: true,
   },
   ...
});
```

For more information, see the [SchemaStringOptions](https://mongoosejs.com/docs/api/schemastringoptions.html#SchemaStringOptions.prototype.lowercase)
page in the Mongoose API documentation.
